gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/418)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/418)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ رِزْقُهُ فِي التِّجَارَةِ وَ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ رِزْقُهُ فِي السَّيْفِ وَ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ رِزْقُهُ فِي لِسَانِهِ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from one of his companions, from Ibrahim Bin Abdul Hameed, from Al Waleed Bin Sabeeh who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘From the people there is one whose sustenance is in the business, and from them is one whose sustenance is in his sword, and from them is one whose sustenance is in his tongue’.
en.sarwar: 5. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from certain persons of our people from Ibrahim ibn ‘ Abd al-Hamid from al-Walid ibn Sabiyh who has said the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘The sustenance of certain people is in trading, others have it in their sword and certain ones in their tongue.”’
